R-01.title: mTLS federation peer authentication
R-01.notes: Threat model marks R-01 mitigated by TLS 1.3 floor, required client certificates, and SAN/entity_uri binding.

R-02.title: Per-principal rate limits
R-02.notes: Covers fact-write, recall, federation-pull, and related token-bucket quota dimensions.

R-03.title: API key max-age enforcement
R-03.notes: Current mitigation includes enforced expiry/max-age plus Phase B Argon2id API-key hashing. Legacy SHA-256 rows verify during the v0.9.x migration window and opportunistically rehash to Argon2id with an api_key_rehashed audit event.

R-06.title: Federation replay protection fuzz coverage
R-06.notes: Evidence focuses on fuzz and nonce-cache survival tests named by the threat model.

R-09.title: General-purpose audit log for reads and admin actions
R-09.notes: Registry entry captures audit-log presence and tests; operational log monitoring remains a residual operator responsibility.

R-10.title: Org manifest key-rotation log entries
R-10.notes: Evidence records that key-rotation events are emitted before activation and tied into audit/transparency-log paths.

R-11.title: Container hardening baseline
R-11.notes: CI container-smoke job also verifies non-root runtime, read-only root filesystem, SBOM generation, and seccomp denylist content.

R-12.title: Per-agent quota dimension
R-12.notes: R-12 shares the R-02 rate-limit implementation but is tracked separately because the threat model names the agent-tool surface.

R-14.title: Capability token verb/object validation
R-14.notes: Evidence records current admission validation; broader capability redesign remains tracked by ADR-003 and Phase B issues.

R-19.title: Inbound remote-HLC skew bounds
R-19.notes: Federation ingest rejects far-future and far-past remote HLC wall times, writes peer_hlc_anomaly audit evidence, and leaves rejected facts uninserted.

R-20.title: Cloud embedding poisoning
R-20.notes: Operator opt-in only. The current release keeps cloud embedding out of the default path; no node-layer vector-integrity mitigation is planned for v0.9.x.

R-21.title: Agent feedback-loop worm
R-21.notes: Cross-cutting risk spanning prompt-injection capability separation, read/write graph isolation, and adapter hardening. ADR-018 keeps the canonical risk at protocol level with contributor notes queued for lazy-instruction-discovery and memory-garden-acl security docs.

R-22.title: Release supply-chain integrity
R-22.notes: Sigstore-signed releases, reproducible builds, SBOM publication, and Rekor entries remain v1.0.0rcN release-candidate deliverables.
